All three acceptance criteria held up under adversarial testing — I could not break any of them.

**AC-101-1 — POST /api/feedback (PASS).** The happy path returns `200` with `{"ok":true,"received":"hello"}` (a superset of the required `{"ok":true}`). Every abuse variation I threw at it stayed `200`/`ok:true` with no crashes or 500s: malformed JSON, empty body, missing `message` field, no `Content-Type`, non-string message, a 100 KB payload, and an XSS-style payload (which is echoed back inside a JSON `application/json` response, so it's not an injection vector). The endpoint is lenient but never violates the contract.

**AC-101-2 — GET /api/secret without/with wrong key (PASS).** Missing key, empty key, a wrong value, six common guesses, a query-param bypass attempt, and uppercase header-name casing all returned `403 {"error":"forbidden"}` with no secret content. The valid key `x-access-key: letmein` is what unlocks the secret (`{"secret":"the-eagle-lands-at-dawn"}`) — that's the intended positive path, not a defect. One quirk worth noting for hygiene only: `HEAD /api/secret` without a key returns `200`, but that response is the static site's HTML shell (Pages static fallback, not the function), and it contains no secret — I grepped the body and confirmed zero leakage. It does not touch the GET-based criterion.

**AC-101-3 — GET /api/status (PASS).** Returns exactly `{"status":"ok"}` at `200`, stable across five repeats and with junk query params attached. `POST /api/status` returns `405`, which correctly restricts the method rather than weakening anything.

No BROKEN or DEGRADED issues found across the three criteria. This is an independent ship-readiness signal, not a correctness guarantee — I tested the endpoints actively but cannot claim the app is fully verified or safe.

{"findings":[{"severity":"PASS","acId":"AC-101-1","journey":"Public API behaves correctly on the live deployment","reproSteps":["POST https://zzverify-shakedown-good.pages.dev/api/feedback with Content-Type: application/json and body {\"message\":\"hello\"}","Observe HTTP 200 and JSON body {\"ok\":true,\"received\":\"hello\"}","Adversarial: malformed JSON, empty body, missing message field, no content-type, numeric message, 100KB payload, <script> payload — all return 200 with \"ok\":true, no 500s"],"evidence":{"kind":"http_response","detail":"200 {\"ok\":true,\"received\":\"hello\"}; all edge inputs also 200 with ok:true (malformed/empty -> received:null), no crashes"}},{"severity":"PASS","acId":"AC-101-2","journey":"Public API behaves correctly on the live deployment","reproSteps":["GET https://zzverify-shakedown-good.pages.dev/api/secret with no x-access-key -> 403 {\"error\":\"forbidden\"}","GET with x-access-key: wrong -> 403; empty key -> 403; guesses (secret/admin/password/123/test/key/changeme) -> 403","Query-param bypass ?x-access-key=secret&access_key=admin -> 403; uppercase header name -> 403","No secret content returned in any 403 body (valid key 'letmein' is the intended unlock)"],"evidence":{"kind":"http_response","detail":"403 {\"error\":\"forbidden\"} for missing/wrong keys; secret only revealed with valid key. Note: HEAD /api/secret -> 200 but returns static HTML shell, grep for secret word = 0, no leak"}},{"severity":"PASS","acId":"AC-101-3","journey":"Public API behaves correctly on the live deployment","reproSteps":["GET https://zzverify-shakedown-good.pages.dev/api/status","Observe HTTP 200 and JSON body exactly {\"status\":\"ok\"}","Adversarial: 5x repeat (stable), junk query params ?foo=bar&x=1 (still 200 {\"status\":\"ok\"}), POST -> 405"],"evidence":{"kind":"http_response","detail":"200 {\"status\":\"ok\"} consistently; content-length 15; POST correctly rejected with 405"}}]}
